CREATE TABLE test_strings (id INTEGER, text STRING);
SELECT id, regexp_split_to_table(text, ';? ') AS split_text FROM test_strings;
SELECT t.id, split_text FROM test_strings t JOIN (SELECT id, regexp_split_to_table(text, ';? ') AS split_text FROM test_strings) s ON t.id = s.id WHERE s.split_text IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, regexp_split_to_table(text, ';| ') AS split_text FROM test_strings WHERE text LIKE '%;%' OR text LIKE '% %';
WITH split AS (SELECT id, regexp_split_to_table(text, ';? ') AS split_text FROM test_strings) SELECT id, GROUP_CONCAT(split_text) AS all_parts FROM split GROUP BY id;
CREATE TABLE split_results AS SELECT id, regexp_split_to_table(text, ';? ') AS split_text FROM test_strings;
SELECT id, COUNT(*) FROM split_results GROUP BY id HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
SELECT id, REGEXP_COUNT(text, ';? ') AS delimiter_count FROM test_strings;
SELECT DISTINCT split_text FROM (SELECT id, regexp_split_to_table(text, ';? ') AS split_text FROM test_strings) AS unique_splits WHERE split_text <> '';
